pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 33: rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/genogram.drawio and export_result.sh line 5: DRAWIO_FILE="/home/ga/Desktop/genogram.drawio"
Verified in setup_task.sh line 34: rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/genogram.png and export_result.sh line 6: PNG_FILE="/home/ga/Desktop/genogram.png"
Clinical intake notes in setup_task.sh lines 8-27: Gen I (Arthur, Betty), Gen II (Charles, Diana, Edward), Gen III (Alice, Frank)
Clinical notes identify: Arthur as Grandfather, Charles as Father (Arthur's son), Edward as Uncle (Charles's brother), Frank as Brother
Clinical notes identify: Betty as Grandmother, Diana as Mother, Alice as female patient (referenced as 'she' in notes)
Clinical notes: Arthur 'Diagnosed with HD in his 40s' (setup_task.sh line 16), Charles 'was diagnosed with HD five years ago and is symptomatic' (line 20). All others have no HD diagnosis mentioned.
Clinical notes: Arthur 'Deceased at age 62' (setup_task.sh line 16), Betty 'Deceased at age 85 (natural causes)' (line 17). All other family members listed as 'Living'.
Clinical notes identify 'Patient: Alice V.' (setup_task.sh line 9). Task description (line 6) instructs to mark 'Alice' with '?' to indicate unknown status, confirming Alice is the proband.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a medical genogram for a patient named Alice concerned about Huntington's Disease (HD). The pedigree includes three generations: Generation I (Arthur with HD, Betty unaffected, both deceased), Generation II (Charles with HD, Diana unaffected, Edward unaffected, all living), and Generation III (Alice asymptomatic proband with unknown status, Frank unaffected, both living). Males use squares/rectangles, females use circles/ellipses. Affected individuals (Arthur, Charles) are shown with solid fill; unaffected are white. Deceased individuals (Arthur, Betty) are marked with diagonal lines. Alice is marked with '?' to indicate unknown genetic status. All data is synthetically generated within the task setup.
